Created by

[![Created by: Dean Pike || deanjp](https://gravatar.com/avatar/efb6e19375a6691a97727acd050cf8e18e7530bb3f9e7cef7701c524ccb191cf?r=pg&d=retro&size=200)\\
\\
Dean Pike](https://n8n.io/creators/deanjp/)

Last update

Last update 4 months ago

Categories

- Document Extraction
- AI Summarization

Share

[Share on X](https://x.com/intent/post?url=https%3A%2F%2Fn8n.io%2Fworkflows%2F9549-convert-fathom-meeting-transcripts-to-formatted-google-docs-with-ai-summaries%2F)[Share on LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/sharing/share-offsite/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fn8n.io%2Fworkflows%2F9549-convert-fathom-meeting-transcripts-to-formatted-google-docs-with-ai-summaries%2F)

* * *

_Transcript → AI Analysis → Formatted Doc_

This workflow automatically converts Fathom meeting transcripts into beautifully formatted Google Docs with AI-generated summaries, key points, decisions, and action items.

### Good to know

- Works fully with Fathom free account
- Webhook responds immediately to prevent Fathom timeout and duplicate triggers
- Validates transcript quality (3+ conversation turns) before AI processing to save costs
- Uses Google Gemini API (generous free tier and rate limits, typically enough to avoid paying for API requests, but check latest pricing at [Google AI Pricing](https://ai.google.dev/pricing))
- Creates temporary HTML file that's auto-deleted after conversion

## Who's it for

Individuals or teams using Fathom for meetings who want more control and flexibility with their AI meeting analysis and storage independently of Fathom, as well as automatic, formatted documentation without manual note-taking. Perfect for recurring syncs, client meetings, or interview debriefs.

## How it works

1. Fathom webhook triggers when meeting ends and sends transcript data
2. Validates transcript has meaningful conversation (3+ turns)
3. Google Gemini AI analyzes transcript and generates structured summary (key points, decisions, actions, next steps)
4. Creates formatted HTML with proper styling
5. Uploads to Google Drive and converts to native Google Doc
6. Reduces page margins for readability and deletes temporary HTML file

## Requirements

- Fathom account with API webhook access (available on free tier)
- Google Drive account (OAuth2)
- Google Docs account (OAuth2)
- Google Gemini API key ( [Get free key here](https://makersuite.google.com/app/apikey))

## How to set up

1. Add credentials: Google Drive OAuth2, Google Docs OAuth2, Google Gemini API
2. Copy the webhook URL from the Get Fathom Meeting webhook node (Test URL first, change to Production URL when ready)
3. In Fathom: Settings → API Access → Add → Add webhook URL and select all events including "Transcript"
4. Test with a short meeting, verify Google Doc appears in Drive
5. Activate workflow

## Customizing this workflow

- **Change save location**: Edit "Upload File as HTML" node → update "Parent Folder"
- **Modify AI output**: Edit "AI Meeting Analysis" node → customize the prompt to add/remove sections (e.g., risks, follow-ups, sentiment, etc)
- **Adjust document margins**: Edit "Reduce Page Margins" node → change margin pixel values
- **Add notifications**: E.g. add Slack/Email node after "Convert to Google Doc" to notify team when summary is ready

## **Quick Troubleshooting**

- **"Transcript Present?" fails**: Fathom must send `transcript_merged` with 3+ conversation turns (i.e. only send to Gemini for analysis if there's a genuine transcript)
- **HTML as plain text**: Check "Convert to Google Doc" uses POST to `/copy` endpoint
- **401/403 errors**: Re-authorize Google credentials
- **Inadequate meeting notes**: Edit prompts in "AI Meeting Analysis" node

## Sample File and Storage Output

- [Google Doc meeting notes - sample](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1tCC90dIpgb8NtuOJ_jSTCPn4MxORB-XcvwdeljYzC9w/edit?usp=drive_link)

## Frequently Asked Questions about Fathom + Google Drive integrations

New to automation with Zapier? You're not alone. Here are some answers to common questions about how Zapier works with Fathom and Google Drive

How do I integrate Fathom with Google Drive?

To integrate Fathom with Google Drive, you need to set up a Zap in the Zapier platform, where Fathom is one of the apps and Google Drive is another. You can trigger actions in Google Drive, such as uploading a file or creating a folder based on actions tracked in Fathom.

What triggers are available when integrating Fathom with Google Drive?

Available triggers when integrating Fathom with Google Drive include new page views or completed events in Fathom, which can initiate actions like uploading documents or organizing files in specified folders within your Google Drive.

Can I automate the saving of reports from Fathom to a specific folder in Google Drive?

Yes, you can automate saving reports from Fathom directly into a specific folder in your Google Drive by setting up a Zap. Use specific triggers such as report completion in Fathom to automatically upload it to your designated Google Drive folder.

Is it possible to create new folders in Google Drive based on certain analytics events captured by Fathom?

Certainly, you can create automation that generates new folders in Google Drive whenever specific analytics events are completed or triggered within Fathom. This helps organize data methodically without manual intervention.

What actions can be taken in Google Drive when an event is triggered by Fathom?

Actions that can be taken include uploading files, creating new documents, and organizing files into folders within your existing structure on Google Drive whenever particular events occur or conditions are met within the Fathom analytics platform.

How do we handle authentication for integrating these two apps?

Authentication between these two services generally requires logging into both your Fathom and Google accounts via our platform to authorize data exchange securely. This ensures safe execution of tasks and access control.

Are there any limitations when using this integration between Fathom and Google Drive?

While this integration allows for extensive automation, one limitation could be on the number of Zaps or tasks handled per month depending on your subscription plan. Additionally, file size limits may apply during transfers onto Google Drive.

# Navigating the Settings Page

Edited 25 days ago

Looking at the [Settings page](https://fathom.video/customize)? Use this guide to understand everything you can configure.

## Auto-Record Settings

Use the first drop-down at the top of the page to determine which meetings should be auto-captured by Fathom:

- **All Meetings** \- both internal and external meetings will be captured automatically.

- **External Meetings** -Fathom will capture scheduled meetings that include at least one attendee from outside your domain.

- **Internal Meetings** \- Fathom will capture scheduled meetings where all attendees are from your domain.

- **No Meetings** \- all Fathom recordings must be initiated manually.


![](https://usw2.frontkb-cdn.com/attachments/5508513/20480/e192314b-84d2-43b4-aee0-1e8fff6e3ce1.png)

Then, use the next drop-down to determine what should be shared post-meeting:

- **Summary & recording** \- both your AI-generated meeting summary and a link to the meeting recording will be automatically shared with attendees.

- **Summary only** \- your AI-generated meeting summary will be automatically shared with attendees. Attendees will not automatically have access to the recording.

- **Nothing** \- attendees will not get a summary or recording of the meeting.


![](https://usw2.frontkb-cdn.com/attachments/5508513/20480/360f5e72-7f6e-46ef-9322-6bc565dcb398.png)

## Video Conferencing

Use these settings to ensure you're connected to Zoom, Google Meet, and/or Microsoft Teams so Fathom can join your meetings.

You can also use the toggles to determine your desired auto-recording behavior for **unscheduled (impromptu)** **meetings** in Zoom or Google Meet.

## Premium Features (Premium and Team plans only)

- **Zapier** \- enable our integration with Zapier. For more about this, check out [this page](https://help.fathom.video/en/articles/450432). **Now available to all users!**

- **Bot Name** \- the name your Fathom notetaker will go by when it joins meetings. You can also **reset** the bot name to the default **(NAME's Fathom Notetaker)** at any time.


![](https://usw2.frontkb-cdn.com/attachments/5508513/20480/85356022-a6e4-4f8c-9e2c-341226dc7f7d.png)

- **Auto-Generate Action Items** \- when this is toggled on, Fathom will automatically extract any action items discussed on your meetings and add them to the Action Items section on a call page.

- **Default Meeting Summary Template** \- choose from our list of meeting templates to set a default for all external meetings captured with Fathom.


![](https://usw2.frontkb-cdn.com/attachments/5508513/20480/b9e65882-c907-4bc4-93a8-3255dbad6660.png)

- **Recording Notification Banner** \- lets attendees know that Fathom is recording and taking notes through a banner image in your meeting:


![](https://usw2.frontkb-cdn.com/attachments/5508513/20480/b21c43b1-0251-4ae3-be26-03a831648c54.png)

## Integrations

Use these settings to ensure you're connected to Slack and either **Salesforce**, **HubSpot**, or **Close**.

## Options

- **Auto Request Recording Consent**\- toggle whether Fathom should automatically send a consent email to meeting attendees. For more on when consent emails are sent and to whom, check out [this article](https://help.fathom.video/en/articles/294272).

- **Make external meetings visible to your team by default** (Team Plans only) - a user-level setting that controls who on your Team has access to your calls. Turning this on makes your calls visible to everyone at your organization in your Team account.







### **Important Note:**



With this setting enabled, **internal calls will not be shared automatically**. Each internal call will need to be manually shared one by one.


## Fathom Apps

Use this section as a quick way to audit that you've installed our Desktop App, Zoom App, and/or Chrome Extension.

## Highlight Options

Use this section to configure the highlight types available to you in your account. This is a user-level setting and will only apply to your Fathom account.

Learn more about Highlights [here](https://help.fathom.video/en/categories/65856).

web.title: Google Drive Integration - Bynder Support
web.description: Navigate to · Click Browse Integrations in the left sidebar then Configure in the Google Drive section. · Enter a Name for this configuration.
web.markdown:
[Submit a request](https://support.bynder.com/hc/en-us/requests/new)

![](https://assets.zendesk.com/hc/assets/default_avatar.png)

[Sign in](https://webdamdb.zendesk.com/auth/v2/login/normal?return_to=https%3A%2F%2Fsupport.bynder.com "Opens a dialog")

## Summary

[Google Drive](https://www.google.com/drive/) is a cloud-based storage solution that allows you to save files online and access them anywhere from any smartphone, tablet, or computer. You can use Drive on your computer or mobile device to securely upload files and edit them online.

By integrating your Bynder portal and Google Drive account, you are able to seamlessly connect assets between the two by automatically moving assets from your Google Drive account to Bynder, without downloading from Google Drive and re-uploading to Bynder. Non-Bynder users can add assets to a folder in Google Drive to have them automatically uploaded to your DAM.

This integration is meant to serve as a ‘hot folder’ for other teams at your organization to drop in files, then those files can be automatically pushed to Bynder. If you have a large amount of content on Google Drive that you would like to migrate to Bynder, we recommend leveraging our [Media Import](https://support.bynder.com/hc/en-us/articles/360017366640) option.

## Who?

This feature/solution requires your Customer Success Contact to enable, but then individual permissions can be done by the Bynder Admin.

Don't yet have Bynder? [Start Here](https://www.bynder.com/en/pricing/)!

Once this feature has been enabled users with the _Portal Settings_ permission can configure this integration.

For more details on configuration, please refer to [Integrations Configuration: Recommended Permissions and Best Practices](https://support.bynder.com/hc/en-us/articles/35238130992274).

## How?

### Setting up the Integration

01. Navigate to![brand_guidelines_image_settings.png](https://support.bynder.com/hc/article_attachments/10641418736914)**Settings** \> **Advanced****settings**\> **Portal****settings**.
02. Click **Browse****Integrations** in the left sidebar then **Configure** in the **Google****Drive** section.
    - On this page, you will also see all the other Integration Hub options that are part of the [Standard Integrations Hub](https://support.bynder.com/hc/en-us/sections/15488109290642-Integration-Hub) package.
    - If you have purchased just one Google Drive connection, please select the Google Drive option. If you have the Standard Integrations Hub option as part of your contract, this allows you to have up to five integrations configured from this page. You are able to see how many connections you have available:
      ![](https://support.bynder.com/hc/article_attachments/36427188983314)
    - Please note that a ‘configuration’ means setting up one of these connections one time. If you set up the Google Drive connector three times to connect three folders, that counts as three configurations.
03. Enter a **Name** for this configuration.
04. If you've already configured a Bynder connection, it will be selected. If not, select **New Authentication** to add your Bynder credentials:
    - **Your authentication name:** Enter a name for the authentication.
    - **Domain**: Enter your unique domain by copying the URL of your Bynder portal (including https://).
05. Click **Create**, then **Next**.
06. On the **Upload Options** page, use the **Filetype Filter** to select whether you want to allow all file types, blacklist or whitelist certain filetypes. If you choose to set up a blacklist or whitelist, an additional text field will appear below where you can add which file extensions you want to block or allow.
07. On the bottom of the page, select the **Send to Waiting Room** check box if you would like all assets from Google Drive to be sent to the Waiting Room for approval. Please note that each file will be loaded separately to the Waiting Room and will not come in batches.
08. On the **Google Drive Setup** page, select **New Authentication** to add your Google Drive credentials, then click **Create**.
09. Click **Select****Sync****Folder** to choose which folder you would like to sync with Bynder.
    - All assets uploaded to this folder will automatically be uploaded to the Bynder Asset Bank.
    - If the Select Sync Folder menu does not load or you cannot find your folder within the dropdown, uncheck the **Folder not listed** checkbox. Under that check box, enter in the Google Drive folder URL or folder ID in the **Enter folder ID or UR** L text field.
10. **Include Sub-folders:**You can choose whether sub-folders in the selected folder will also sync to Bynder.    - Check the box if you would like assets in the sub-folders to be synced to Bynder.
    - Leave the box unchecked if only assets uploaded to the folder selected should be uploaded to Bynder.
11. **Static Metaproperties**: You can automatically add certain metaproperty values to all assets that are synced from Google Drive. For example, if you know that you’ll only be syncing images from Google Drive, you can set the Asset Type metaproperty to Images. Please note that this is not a dynamic mapping; all assets coming from Box to Bynder will be tagged with the fields you select here.    1. Click **Choose an option** under **Bynder Metaproperty** and select a field from the dropdown.
    2. In the **Value** section, enter the option that the synced assets should be tagged with.
    3. Click **Add a new mapping** to add additional metaproperties.
    4. Click **Next**, review the information, then click **Finish**.

### Note

- The keyword tags will include 'Google Drive' and the Google Drive file ID, and the description will include a link to the synced Google Drive folder.
- Your [portal](https://support.bynder.com/hc/en-us/articles/360013936359) can accept any supported file type. Google native files (e.g., Google Sheets, Google Docs, Google Slides) will convert to the equivalent Microsoft files and be uploaded.

### Using the Integration

After configuration, all selected assets in your Google Drive folder will upload to your portal’s Asset Bank after about 2 hours. Moving forward, any assets added or moved to the selected folder (or sub-folders) in the configured Google Drive account will automatically be uploaded to the Asset Bank after about 2 hours.

If you delete an asset from your Google Drive account, it will not automatically be removed from your Bynder DAM. If you delete a Google Drive asset from your Bynder DAM it will remain in your Google Drive account.

If you would like an additional Google Drive folder synced, you will need to configure another Google Drive workflow.

Here, you can learn how to leverage metaproperty permissions to manage visibility, control access rights, or create an approval workflow.

Editing the Integration

If you would like to disable the integration, update your synced folder, or edit your Google Drive credentials, you can **edit** the configuration

1. Navigate to **Settings** >**Advanced****settings** >**Portal****settings**.
2. Click **Active****Integrations** in the left sidebar, then **Edit** in the section of the connection that you would like to update.
3. Make your changes, then click **Save**.

## FAQs

**Which file types are supported?**

Your [portal](https://support.bynder.com/hc/en-us/articles/360013936359) can accept any supported file type. Google native files (e.g., Google Sheets, Google Docs, Google Slides) will be converted to the equivalent Microsoft files and uploaded.

**What happens to the asset if I delete it from my Bynder portal (or vice versa)?**

If you delete an asset from your Bynder portal, the asset will remain in your Google Drive account, and if you delete an asset from your Google Drive account, it will remain in your Bynder portal.

**Does the integration support versions?**

At this time it does not support versioning.

**Once assets are added to my synced Google Drive folder, how long will it take for them to appear in Bynder?**

The updates will run on a 2-hour interval, so your Google Drive assets will be pushed to your Bynder portal every 2 hours.

**Is a specific Google Drive account type required for the integration?**

No, any account type will work for the integration (such as personal, enterprise, etc).

**What if we have multiple Google Drive folders that we want to sync?**

You can also choose to sync all subfolders within a selected Google Drive folder. If you’d like to sync a different folder, it will need to be configured and connected.

**What is the intended use case and best practice for this integration?**

Due to metadata mapping limitations, this may not be ideal for a large migration of content from Google Drive to Bynder, but instead an ongoing push of content from a Google Drive ‘hot folder’ into Bynder.

## Related Articles

[Integrations Configuration: Recommended Permissions and Best Practices](https://support.bynder.com/hc/en-us/articles/35238130992274)

## Overview

Records, transcribes & summarizes meetings FREE

Install this Fathom for Google Meet Chrome extension to unlock:

\\* Ability to use Fathom on any Google Meet - without it you have to make sure to join the meeting from the Fathom desktop app
\\* Embedded recording controls right inside Google Meet, so you never have to dig for the desktop app
\\* Instant join for any Meet link you click — even unscheduled calls

ABOUT FATHOM

It’s AI notetaking for the way you work. Fathom captures, transcribes, and summarizes your Zoom, Google Meet, and Microsoft Teams meetings, now available bot-free, so you can stay fully present in the conversation and let the notes take themselves.

🆓 Free forever. Unlimited recording, transcription, and storage. Get set up in minutes.
🥇 #1 rated AI Notetaker on G2, used at 300K+ companies.
🔒 Enterprise-grade security: SOC 2 Type II, GDPR, and HIPAA compliant, with SSO/SCIM available.

KEY FEATURES

⚡ Instant, Shockingly Accurate Summaries: The moment your call ends, you get a clean transcript, a high-quality summary, and AI-generated action items delivered straight to your inbox. Turn a 30-minute call into a 1-minute read.

🪐 Ask Fathom – Across Every Meeting You've Ever Had: A ChatGPT-style interface for your entire meeting history. Ask things like "What did the customer say about pricing last quarter?" or "Summarize every conversation about the Acme deal" and get instant, cited answers.

🛸 Bot-Free Capture: Record meetings without a bot joining as a visible participant. Stay present, stay private, and skip the "who invited the notetaker?" conversation.

🤖 ChatGPT & Claude Integrations: Bring your meeting intelligence into the AI tools you already use. Connect Fathom to ChatGPT or Claude and pull insights, draft follow-ups, or analyze patterns across calls.

🎯 AI Scorecards & Coaching: Auto-grade calls against custom criteria — perfect for sales, customer success, and interview workflows. Managers coach with confidence; reps level up faster.

🔥 Auto-Updates Salesforce, HubSpot, and Asana: No more post-call data entry. Fathom logs the call, writes the summary, updates the right fields, and creates tasks in your project tools — automatically.

🔔 Keyword & Topic Alerts: Get notified the moment a competitor, feature request, or critical topic gets mentioned in any meeting across your team.

🙏 Easily Share Highlights & Clips: Show, don't tell. Clip key moments from any call and share them with teammates, prospects, or leadership in a couple of clicks.

🔌 Works with the tools you already use: Slack, Notion, Gmail, Zapier, plus a Public API and MCP server for custom workflows.

# [FREE AI Note Taking App for Google Meet | Fathom AI Tutorial](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=gNHJYIXJwOg)

**Visibility**: Public
**Uploaded by**: [Lui Iacobellis](https://www.youtube.com/@LuiIacobellis)
**Uploaded at**: 2025-08-09
**Published at**: 
**Length**: 03:53
**Views**: 3489
**Likes**: 23
**Category**: Science & Technology

## Description

```
👉 Sign up for your free Fathom account here:  https://bit.ly/3U9SSCR


Want to automatically take notes during your Google Meet calls—without lifting a finger? In this video, I’ll show you how to use Fathom AI, a free and powerful note-taking tool that transcribes, summarizes, and captures key takeaways from your meetings using AI.

Whether you're a student, professional, or remote worker, this tool makes your meetings more productive by taking smart notes for you, completely hands-free.

✅ What You’ll Learn
• How to install and set up Fathom AI
• How to automatically record and transcribe Google Meet meetings
• How Fathom AI generates summaries and highlights action items
• How to view, edit, and export your meeting notes

🧠 Powered by AI, Fathom is one of the best free note-taking apps for Google Meet—perfect for busy people who want to focus on the conversation, not the keyboard.


-MORE ABOUT ME-

Become a Channel Member & Get Access To Awesome Perks:
https://bit.ly/3wEuXQq

❓Have a question❓ 📢 Want to promote your product or service on my channel or newsletter 📢 🎓 Looking for coaching or training 🎓 If so, get in touch 📧 with me here 🔗 https://academy.luigiiacobellis.com/contact

LETS BE FRIENDSI
🔔Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/luigiiacobellis/
🔔LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/liacobe/

🔔TikTok: https://www.tiktok.com/@luigiiacobellis

🔔Website: https://academy.luigiiacobellis.com/
🔔Linktree: https://linktr.ee/Liacobellis

GOT A VIDEO IDEA? FILL OUT THIS FORM:
https://bit.ly/3yjEewm


📧 Note that I may earn a small commission at no additional cost to you from purchases made using these links.
```

## Transcript

To use AI to automatically take notes
during Google Meet calls, start by going
to the Fathom home screen and click on
the settings button. Next, choose your
recording preference. Automatically
record all meetings. Record only
internal meetings or manually start
recordings as needed. In this example,
Fathom is set to automatically record
all meetings. Then adjust your sharing
preferences based on your needs.
Note, you can sign up for a free Fathom
AI account using the link in the
description below. Next, scroll down and
download the Fathom browser extension.
Click get, then add extension.
Once the extension is installed, sign
into your Fathom account using your
username and password. After logging in,
you'll see a confirmation that Fathom is
connected and ready to use. Once setup
is complete, the Fathom bot will
automatically attempt to join your
meeting as soon as it starts. Keep in
mind that the bot must be admitted into
the call. If you're not the organizer,
the organizer will see a prompt like
this and will need to let the bot in.
Once admitted, the Fathom bot will begin
recording and taking notes based on your
configured settings. You'll see the
Fathom icon appear in your Google Meet
controls to indicate that it's recording
and the bots tile will also confirm it's
active. Before we jump into the Fathom
platform, listen to this short clip from
the meeting. This is what was said.
Soon, you'll see how Fathom captured and
summarized it.
>> Three targets are about 70% complete.
Great job so far, but we still have a
few items to lock in before the 15th.
Here are today's key action items. If
you're part of the testing group, check
your inbox. Calendar invites are going
out this afternoon. The feedback survey
will be sent out on Thursday. It'll only
take 2 minutes. Please complete it by
Monday. That's it for my end. After the
meeting ends, a popup will confirm that
Fathom successfully recorded the
session.
Back on the Fathom home screen. Your
recorded meeting will appear as a tile.
To access the notes, simply click on the
tile. Start by hovering over the video
and clicking to play back the recording.
Next, choose a template that best fits
the type of meeting. These templates
help format and summarize the content
more effectively. In this example, I'll
select the general template. Fathom will
now begin generating a summary of the
meeting. Once complete, it will display
the meeting's purpose, key takeaways,
main topics, and more.
To view the full transcript, click on
the transcript tab. This shows the
speechtoext output and highlights any
identified action items. You'll also
find action items listed in the right
hand panel. You can click on any of them
to edit or update the text.
Finally, Fathom includes a built-in
generative agent that you can prompt for
insights about the meeting. Just click
ask Fathom, type your question, and get
a real-time response.
If you'd like to learn more about Fathom
AI, leave a comment below. And don't
forget, you can sign up for a free
Fathom AI account using the link in the
description.
If you found this video helpful, be sure
to give it a like, drop a comment, and
subscribe for more AI tutorials. And
while you're here, check out these other
videos. You might find them helpful as
